title: What is Regex?
concept: Regular Expressions (Regex) are search patterns used to match and extract specific character combinations from text strings.

exercises.prompt: What does the term 'Regex' stand for?
exercises.explain: Regex stands for 'Regular Expression'. It is a powerful tool used for matching patterns in text.

cards.title: Supercharge Your Searches
cards.body: Have you ever tried using 'Find and Replace' (Ctrl+F) in a document, but wished you could search for *any* email address or *any* phone number instead of just one specific name? That is the superpower of Regex!

cards.title: What is Regex?
cards.body: A Regular Expression (often shortened to **Regex**) is a special sequence of characters that forms a search pattern. Instead of searching for an exact word, you search for a pattern—like 'any three numbers followed by a letter'. It helps you find, edit, or manage text quickly.

cards.title: The Metal Detector
cards.body: Think of regex like a high-tech metal detector at the beach. Instead of digging through the sand by hand to find coins, keys, or rings, you set your detector to specifically scan for metallic shapes. Regex lets you scan a massive wall of text and instantly pull out only the specific patterns you asked for, like email addresses or phone numbers.

cards.title: Regex in Action
cards.body: Imagine you have a list of user sign-ups and want to check if they entered valid zip codes (exactly 5 numbers). Instead of writing complex code, you can use the regex pattern `^[0-9]{5}$`. This instantly highlights codes like `90210` and flags errors like `ABCDE`.

cards.title: Myth: It's a New Language
cards.body: Many beginners think Regex is a whole new programming language they need weeks to master. In reality, Regex is just a search syntax supported by almost every programming language (Python, JavaScript, Java). Once you learn the basic symbols, you can use them anywhere!

cards.title: Exact Match vs. Pattern Match
cards.body: See how standard search compares to using Regex pattern matching.

right.text: Searches for patterns, like 'any three-letter word ending in -at' (matches cat, bat, hat).

left.text: Searches only for exact matches, like finding the word 'cat' but missing 'bat' or 'hat'.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following tasks is best suited for using Regex?

options.text: Finding all email addresses in a document
Adding colors and styling to web pages

exercises.explain: Regex is designed to find patterns in text, which makes it perfect for finding email addresses, dates, or phone numbers. It doesn't play music or format layout styles.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Regex only works inside the Python programming language.
exercises.explain: Regex is supported by almost all major coding languages including Python, JavaScript, SQL, C++, and Java.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the definition of Regex.
exercises.sentence: Regex is a search string that defines a search pattern to find matching text.
exercises.explain: Regex stands for Regular Expression, which is a search pattern used to match specific characters in a string.

exercises.prompt: Imagine an AI wrote a pattern to find valid email addresses. Spot the option that does NOT fit the pattern:
exercises.explain: The text 'hello@website' is missing the domain extension (like '.com' or '.org'), so it doesn't match the standard email pattern. The other two options are valid emails.

exercises.prompt: You are building a spam filter for a chat app and want to use Regex to find forbidden words. What exactly is a Regex?

options.text: A programming language used to build databases
A tool that automatically translates English to Spanish
A search pattern used to find specific character combinations in text
A physical device used to clean keyboard dust

exercises.explain: Great job! A regex pattern acts as a set of rules or a template that scanner programs use to inspect text and find matches, similar to looking for words starting with a specific letter.
